title_en: Ibn Rajab al-Hanbali and the Science of Legal Maxims
title_ar: ابن رجب الحنبلي وعلم القواعد الفقهية
content_en:
Imam Zayn al-Din Abd al-Rahman ibn Ahmad ibn Rajab al-Hanbali (736–795 AH / 1335–1393 CE) was born in Baghdad and received his scholarly formation across the major centers of Islamic learning in his era — Damascus, Jerusalem, Mecca, and Egypt. He was a master of multiple disciplines: hadith, Hanbali jurisprudence, Islamic ethics, and the emerging science of legal maxims (al-qawa'id al-fiqhiyyah). He is remembered as one of the most distinguished Hanbali scholars of the eighth Islamic century, a figure of remarkable depth and breadth whose works continue to be studied and taught.

His Al-Qawa'id al-Fiqhiyyah — more formally titled Al-Qawa'id fi al-Fiqh al-Islami (The Legal Maxims in Islamic Jurisprudence) — is a collection and analysis of 160 legal maxims that organize the jurisprudential principles underlying Hanbali fiqh. It is one of the earliest and most comprehensive works in the genre of qawa'id fiqhiyyah literature, a discipline that sought to identify the general principles from which specific rulings derive and to which they can be referred for consistency and coherence.

The science of legal maxims (qawa'id fiqhiyyah) emerged from the recognition that Islamic jurisprudence — with its thousands of specific rulings across every dimension of human life — needed organizing principles to give it systematic coherence. The maxims are not legal rulings themselves but generalizations that describe the logic underlying groups of rulings. The five grand maxims (al-qawa'id al-kulliyyah al-khams), accepted across all four major schools of law, are the apex of this system: 'Actions are judged by intentions,' 'Certainty is not removed by doubt,' 'Hardship brings ease,' 'Harm must be removed,' and 'Custom is an authority.' Around these five, hundreds of more specific maxims elaborate the logic of Islamic law across different domains.

Ibn Rajab's work presents 160 maxims — far more than the five universals — with explanations of their scope, their exceptions, their relationship to hadith and Quranic principles, and their application to specific fiqh problems. His approach is characteristic of the Hanbali tradition: careful attention to the textual sources (Quran and Sunnah), awareness of the positions of earlier scholars (particularly the Hanbali imams from Ahmad ibn Hanbal through Ibn Qudamah and beyond), and intellectual rigor in distinguishing where maxims apply without exception and where they admit of qualifications.

The work is essential reading for anyone seeking to understand Hanbali jurisprudence systematically rather than as a collection of discrete rulings. It reveals the internal logic of the school — why Hanbali fiqh rules as it does, what principles explain the pattern of its decisions, and how different rulings across different domains (purity, prayer, commerce, family law, criminal law) share underlying logic. This systematic perspective is what distinguishes a jurist from a mere rule-follower, and it is what Ibn Rajab's qawa'id were designed to cultivate.
title_id: Ibn Rajab al-Hanbali dan Ilmu Kaidah-Kaidah Fikih
content_id:
Imam Zayn al-Din Abd al-Rahman ibn Ahmad ibn Rajab al-Hanbali (736-795 H / 1335-1393 M) lahir di Baghdad dan mendapat pembentukan keilmuan di pusat-pusat utama pembelajaran Islam di eranya — Damaskus, Yerusalem, Mekah, dan Mesir. Ia menguasai berbagai disiplin: hadis, fikih Hanbali, etika Islam, dan ilmu kaidah-kaidah fikih yang sedang berkembang (al-qawa'id al-fiqhiyyah). Ia dikenang sebagai salah satu ulama Hanbali paling terkemuka di abad kedelapan Islam, seorang tokoh dengan kedalaman dan keluasan yang luar biasa yang karyanya terus dipelajari dan diajarkan.

Al-Qawa'id al-Fiqhiyyah-nya — lebih formal berjudul Al-Qawa'id fi al-Fiqh al-Islami (Kaidah-Kaidah dalam Fikih Islam) — adalah kumpulan dan analisis dari 160 kaidah fikih yang mengorganisir prinsip-prinsip yurisprudensial yang mendasari fikih Hanbali. Ini adalah salah satu karya paling awal dan paling komprehensif dalam genre literatur qawa'id fiqhiyyah, sebuah disiplin yang berusaha mengidentifikasi prinsip-prinsip umum dari mana hukum-hukum spesifik berasal dan kepada mana mereka dapat dirujuk untuk konsistensi dan koherensi.

Ilmu kaidah-kaidah fikih muncul dari pengakuan bahwa yurisprudensi Islam — dengan ribuan hukum spesifiknya di setiap dimensi kehidupan manusia — memerlukan prinsip-prinsip pengorganisasian untuk memberinya koherensi sistematis. Kaidah-kaidah tersebut bukan hukum-hukum fikih itu sendiri tetapi generalisasi-generalisasi yang menggambarkan logika yang mendasari kelompok-kelompok hukum. Lima kaidah besar (al-qawa'id al-kulliyyah al-khams), yang diterima di semua empat mazhab utama, adalah puncak sistem ini: "Segala perkara tergantung pada niatnya," "Keyakinan tidak dihilangkan oleh keraguan," "Kesulitan mendatangkan kemudahan," "Bahaya harus dihilangkan," dan "Adat adalah hukum."

Karya Ibn Rajab menyajikan 160 kaidah — jauh lebih banyak dari lima kaidah universal — dengan penjelasan tentang ruang lingkupnya, pengecualian-pengecualiannya, hubungannya dengan prinsip-prinsip hadis dan Al-Qur'an, dan penerapannya pada masalah-masalah fikih spesifik. Pendekatannya khas dari tradisi Hanbali: perhatian cermat terhadap sumber-sumber tekstual (Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah), kesadaran akan posisi-posisi ulama-ulama sebelumnya (khususnya imam-imam Hanbali dari Ahmad ibn Hanbal melalui Ibn Qudamah dan seterusnya), dan ketegasan intelektual dalam membedakan di mana kaidah-kaidah berlaku tanpa pengecualian dan di mana mereka mengakui kualifikasi.

Karya ini merupakan bacaan wajib bagi siapa pun yang ingin memahami fikih Hanbali secara sistematis daripada sebagai kumpulan hukum-hukum yang terpisah. Karya ini mengungkapkan logika internal mazhab — mengapa fikih Hanbali memutusfkan sebagaimana yang dilakukannya dalam domain tertentu, prinsip-prinsip apa yang menjelaskan pola keputusannya, dan bagaimana hukum-hukum yang berbeda di berbagai domain (kesucian, shalat, perdagangan, hukum keluarga, hukum pidana) berbagi logika yang mendasarinya.
